title: Live-HA smoke test: install integration, add spots, exercise config + prefs flows
description: Deploy custom_components/swelligence into a running HA (copy to /config/custom_components, restart), add the Christchurch spots, walk the config flow + the new per-spot preference flow (offshore dirs), confirm sensors/binary_sensors appear per (spot x sport) and scores match the standalone validation. Also smoke-test the ai_task path with a configured agent. Nothing has been loaded in live HA yet — only standalone scoring is verified.

title: Open-Meteo marine returns nearest-coastal data for inland/sheltered spots — suppress by water_type
description: Validation (c1v.1) proved the marine API returns the nearest COASTAL grid cell even for inland coords: New Forest Water Park (inland lake) got phantom wave 0.62m + sea-temp 19.2C from open sea ~11mi away; Christchurch Harbour (flat/sheltered) got 0.64m from the open-sea grid, over-penalising SUP/swim/wake. has_marine cannot detect this. Fix: thread spot water_type into the provider/coordinator and null wave/swell/water-temp fields when water_type=inland; consider an attenuation factor for sheltered (harbour) spots. Strategic fix at provider level, not per-call site.
notes: Fixed via water_type policy (sea/sheltered/inland) + shared policy.py. Re-validated: harbour wind-only+temp kept, NFWP fully suppressed.

title: M0: scaffold integration foundation
description: Provider abstraction + Open-Meteo, scoring, sport profiles, coordinator, config/options flow, sensors, AI Task hook, HACS metadata, docs. Delivered in initial commits.

title: M1: per-spot preference overrides UI (offshore wind dirs, wind/wave windows)
description: Options-flow UI to override SportProfile defaults per spot. Data model ready via dataclasses.replace on defaults. Highest-value tuning lever = offshore wind directions per spot.
notes: Per-spot overrides implemented: apply_overrides + per-spot profile resolution + 3-step options-flow UI (spot->sport->edit). Offshore-direction path proven standalone (onshore 59 vs offshore 92, same swell). Options-flow UI needs live-HA smoke test (see follow-up).

title: Validate scoring against a live Open-Meteo spot
description: Add one real spot, pull a live forecast, sanity-check scores/verdicts and tune default sport profiles before building more UI.
notes: Pulled live Open-Meteo forecasts for all 6 Christchurch spots and ran the scorer (scripts/validate_spots.py). Wind sports + best-window scan validated as sensible. Surfaced 1 bug (marine-data-for-inland/sheltered, filed) + profile tuning (filed). Runner committed for reuse.

title: Swelligence v1 — water/wind-sports suitability integration
description: HA custom integration scoring favourite spots for multiple sports from forecast data, with optional AI Task semantic verdicts. M0 scaffold complete (provider abstraction + Open-Meteo, deterministic scoring, sport profiles, coordinator, config/options flow, sensors, AI hook). See docs/SCOPE.md.

title: Sizing model + calibration (sizing.py): weight/wind -> ideal kite/wing size
description: Pure, calibratable sizing functions feeding M8 quiver-aware scoring. Defaults C_kite~2.25, C_wing~1.0; user-tunable. Unit-test against known rider/wind->size points. No I/O.
notes: Pure sizing.py + 15 tests. ideal_size (inverse weight/wind model) + recommend_kit (nearest-owned power match: ideal/under/over/no_kit/n-a). Constants calibratable. Unblocks c1v.12.

title: M8: rider personalisation — profile + quiver-aware kite/wing recommendations
description: Personalise powered-sport suitability to the single local rider. RiderProfile (weight kg, optional ability) + one Quiver (owned kite sizes m2, wing sizes m2, optional boards/foils) in config — no multi-user modelling, no per-rider entity multiplication. Calibratable sizing model (sizing.py): ideal_kite_m2 ~ C_kite*weight/wind_kn (C_kite~2.25), ideal_wing_m2 ~ C_wing*weight/wind_kn (C_wing~1.0). Quiver-aware scoring: pick nearest owned size to ideal -> power-match factor (ideal=full, off-size=under/over penalty, nothing usable=cap low). Output per powered-sport sensor: recommended_size, owned_size_to_rig, power verdict. Feed rider context to the LLM verdict too. See docs/SCOPE.md Personalisation section.

title: Calibrate sport profiles: graduated wave factor + softer gust handling
description: From c1v.1 validation: (1) wave factor is binary within range so 0.6m reads 'epic' for surf — ramp toward an ideal wave height per sport instead of full credit anywhere in [min,max]. (2) Light-wind sports (SUP, sea swim, wakeboard) hard-fail to a 30 cap the instant a gust exceeds a low ceiling (16-18kn); consider a graduated gust penalty rather than the blunt 30-floor. Revisit gust_max + wave windows per profile.
notes: Graduated wave factor (wave_ideal_m + comfort plateau) and graduated gust penalty (dropped from hard-fail cap). Re-validated across all 6 spots: surf no longer epic at 0.6m, light-wind sports sensible on gusty wind, wind sports unaffected by small chop.

title: M7: tests + CI (pytest scoring/providers, hassfest, HACS)
description: Unit-test pure scoring (bands, hard-fails, direction wrap, missing fields); provider normalisation vs recorded fixtures; HA config-flow harness. hassfest/HACS CI already scaffolded.
notes: 35-test pytest suite (scorer/overrides/policy/provider-normalisation), HA-free via stub package. CI runs tests+hassfest+HACS. Config-flow tests needing HA harness deferred to live-HA smoke test (c1v.14).

title: M4: additional providers (Windy, Stormglass, UKHO tides)
description: Implement ForecastProvider for Windy (keyed) and Stormglass (marine+tide); UKHO tides as a UK tide overlay. Register in PROVIDERS.

title: M3: custom Lovelace card — spot x sport suitability matrix
description: Green/amber/red matrix with best-in-N-hours hint and AI summary tooltip.

title: M2: add spots by place name (Open-Meteo geocoding API)
description: Geocode a place name to coordinates in the add_spot flow instead of raw lat/lon entry.

title: M6: notification blueprint — alert when spot good for sport
description: Blueprint firing when a (spot x sport) suitability binary sensor goes on.

title: M5: tide awareness in scoring for tide-dependent spots
description: Factor tide state/height into scoring where the spot is tide-dependent. Depends on a tide provider.
